metadata.competency_question: Identify the substrate that accumulates in Fabry disease and explain why

answer.reason_for_accumulation: Loss-of-function mutations in the GLA gene (Xq22.1) reduce or abolish alpha-galactosidase A enzyme activity. This lysosomal hydrolase normally cleaves the terminal alpha-galactosyl moiety from Gb3. Without functional enzyme, Gb3 progressively accumulates in lysosomes of vascular endothelial cells, podocytes, cardiomyocytes, and dorsal root ganglion neurons, driving the multi-organ pathology of Fabry disease.

enzyme_deficient.normal_function: Catalyzes the hydrolysis of glycosphingolipids and participates in their degradation in the lysosome

genes.name: galactosidase alpha
genes.role: Causal gene — loss-of-function mutations cause Fabry disease

genes.name: alpha 1,4-galactosyltransferase
genes.role: Synthesizes Gb3 by adding alpha-1,4-galactose to lactosylceramide; upstream biosynthetic enzyme

genes.name: hexosaminidase subunit alpha
genes.role: Co-pathway lysosomal hydrolase (deficient in Tay-Sachs disease); STRING interaction score 0.939

genes.name: hexosaminidase subunit beta
genes.role: Co-pathway lysosomal hydrolase (deficient in Sandhoff disease); STRING interaction score 0.941

genes.name: arylsulfatase A
genes.role: Co-pathway lysosomal hydrolase (deficient in metachromatic leukodystrophy); STRING interaction score 0.974

compounds.name: Agalsidase beta (Fabrazyme)
compounds.role: Enzyme replacement therapy — recombinant alpha-galactosidase A

compounds.name: Agalsidase alfa (Replagal)
compounds.role: Enzyme replacement therapy — recombinant alpha-galactosidase A

compounds.name: Migalastat (Galafold)
compounds.role: Pharmacological chaperone — stabilizes amenable mutant GLA protein

compounds.name: Lucerastat
compounds.role: Substrate reduction therapy — inhibits glucosylceramide synthase

compounds.name: Voxeralgagene autotemcel
compounds.role: Gene therapy — ex vivo lentiviral vector delivering functional GLA gene

compounds.name: Duvalgagene otiparvovec
compounds.role: Gene therapy — AAV-based in vivo delivery of functional GLA gene

pathways.name: Degradation pathway of sphingolipids, including diseases
pathways.relevance: Primary pathway — maps GLA's role in Gb3 catabolism and related sphingolipidoses

pathways.name: Glycosphingolipid metabolism
pathways.relevance: Biosynthesis pathway — maps A4GALT-catalyzed Gb3 synthesis from lactosylceramide

pathways.name: Sphingolipid metabolism
pathways.relevance: Broader metabolic context for glycosphingolipid processing

NCBI Gene summary, OMIM, clinical phenotype

validation_summary.gene_disease_link: VALIDATED — Open Targets score 0.893, OMIM 301500, NCBI Gene summary explicitly states GLA mutations cause Fabry disease
validation_summary.substrate_identification: VALIDATED — NCBI Gene summary: 'This enzyme predominantly hydrolyzes ceramide trihexoside'; PubChem CID66616222 confirmed as Gb3
validation_summary.mechanism_of_accumulation: VALIDATED — Loss of alpha-galactosidase A activity prevents terminal alpha-galactosyl cleavage from Gb3
validation_summary.drug_mechanisms: VALIDATED — ChEMBL confirms agalsidase beta as Gb3 hydrolytic enzyme and migalastat as GLA stabiliser
validation_summary.trial_nct_ids: VALIDATED — 4/4 NCT IDs verified with full protocol details
